options.panelConfig:
---
anchors:
  thresholds-operstate: &thresholds-operstate
    - color: "#FF3154" # red
      level: 0
    - color: "#4BDD33" # green
      level: 1
  thresholds-traffic: &thresholds-traffic
    - color: "#bec8d2" # grey
      level: 0
    - color: "#4BDD33" # green
      level: 199999
    - color: "#FFFF00" # yellow
      level: 500000
    - color: "#FF8000" # orange
      level: 1000000
    - color: "#FF3154" # red
      level: 5000000
  label-config: &label-config
    separator: replace
    units: bps
    decimalPoints: 1
    valueMappings:
      - valueMax: 199999
        text: ​
      - valueMin: 200000
cellIdPreamble: cell-
cells:
  spine1:e1-1:leaf1:e1-31:
    dataRef: oper-state:clab-srexperts-spine21:ethernet-1/1
    fillColor:
      thresholds: *thresholds-operstate
  link_id:spine1:e1-1:leaf1:e1-31:
    dataRef: clab-srexperts-spine21:ethernet-1/1:out
    label: *label-config
    strokeColor:
      thresholds: *thresholds-traffic
  spine1:e1-2:leaf2:e1-31:
    dataRef: oper-state:clab-srexperts-spine21:ethernet-1/2
    fillColor:
      thresholds: *thresholds-operstate

options.siteConfig:
zoomPanPinch:
  # This optional term defines the array of keys that can be used to enable wheel zoom. There's
  # a clash of interest between panel zoom and dashboard scrolling which is easy to avoid with small
  # SVGs, but becomes more problematic when the SVG is large and covers a lot of the screen. By
  # requiring a combo such as ALT+Scroll this clash can be avoided.
  wheelActivationKeys: ["Alt"]
